title: Cartagena 3-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Cartagena 3-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 3-day trip to Cartagena, Colombia: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Castillo San Felipe de Barajas and Palacio de la Inquisición. Prices from $627 per person (estimate).
highlights: Sunset cocktails atop the city wall at Café del Mar
overview: Spend 3 days discovering Cartagena, Colombia — Hot and humid Caribbean coastal climate year-round, highs around 31-33°C, with a rainier season roughly May to November. December to April — the dry season, with lower humidity and less rain than the rest of the year. This itinerary blends Castillo San Felipe de Barajas, Palacio de la Inquisición, Ciudad Amurallada (Walled City) with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
2 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Castillo San Felipe de Barajas, Palacio de la Inquisición, Ciudad Amurallada (Walled City)
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Cartagena
itinerary.description: Land at Rafael Núñez International Airport (CTG) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Castillo San Felipe de Barajas — Massive 17th-century Spanish fortress overlooking the city. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Cartagena: Palacio de la Inquisición & Islas del Rosario boat tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Palacio de la Inquisición — Museum on the colonial-era Spanish Inquisition. In the afternoon, continue to Islas del Rosario boat tour — Day trip to a coral-reef archipelago. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Museo del Oro Zenú or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to CTG for your departure flight.

travelTips: Walk the city walls near sunset for the best light and cooler temperatures.
Book Islas del Rosario boat tours through a reputable operator rather than a beach tout.
Negotiate politely with street vendors in the Walled City — bartering is expected.
Stay hydrated; the heat and humidity are intense even in the "cooler" dry season.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Lightweight, breathable clothing for the heat and humidity
Strong sun protection — hat, sunscreen, sunglasses
Comfortable sandals for cobblestones and beach transitions
thingsToDo: Mud volcano visit at El Totumo
Islas del Rosario boat day trip
Oceanario aquarium at Islas del Rosario
Horse-and-carriage ride through the Walled City
thingsToAvoid: Wandering outside the Walled City/Getsemaní/Bocagrande tourist areas alone at night.
Booking boat tours from unlicensed operators on the street — verify life jackets and permits.
Underestimating the midday heat — plan strenuous sightseeing for morning or late afternoon.
nightlife: Café del Mar sunset drinks on the wall
Plaza de la Trinidad street parties
instagramSpots: Colorful balconies of the Walled City
familyActivities: Islas del Rosario boat day trip
Oceanario aquarium at Islas del Rosario
Horse-and-carriage ride through the Walled City
adventureActivities: Mud volcano visit at El Totumo
luxuryExperiences: Private sunset sail along the city walls
Suite in a converted colonial mansion hotel
Private catamaran day trip to Islas del Rosario
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset cocktails atop the city wall at Café del Mar
Private beach day at Islas del Rosario
Candlelit rooftop dinner in the Walled City
budgetExperiences: Free wander through the Walled City and Getsemaní
Free sunset walk along the ramparts
Local lunch (menú del día) near Mercado de Bazurto
localEtiquette: The walled city is touristy — friendly but firm negotiation is expected with street vendors.
Modest, light clothing is fine for the heat; swimwear belongs on the beach, not the historic streets.
Greet with a handshake or, among friends, a single cheek kiss.
localTransportGuide: The Walled City and Getsemaní are entirely walkable. For trips further out (Bocagrande, the airport, day-trip departure docks), taxis and rideshare apps are inexpensive and the easiest option — agree on a fare or use the app's estimate before riding.
bestTimeToVisit: December to April — the dry season, with lower humidity and less rain than the rest of the year.
weather: Hot and humid Caribbean coastal climate year-round, highs around 31-33°C, with a rainier season roughly May to November.
visaInformation: Visa-free entry for stays up to 90 days for US, UK, EU, Canadian, and Australian passport holders. Indian nationals need a Colombian e-visa arranged in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~16.5h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Cartagena?
faqs.answer: Visa-free entry for stays up to 90 days for US, UK, EU, Canadian, and Australian passport holders. Indian nationals need a Colombian e-visa arranged in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Cartagena?
faqs.answer: December to April — the dry season, with lower humidity and less rain than the rest of the year. Hot and humid Caribbean coastal climate year-round, highs around 31-33°C, with a rainier season roughly May to November.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Cartagena safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Cartagena is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Cartagena?
faqs.answer: The local currency is COP. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Cartagena is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Sunset cocktails atop the city wall at Café del Mar experience.

reviews.author: Isabella Wang
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Castillo San Felipe de Barajas and Las Bóvedas. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Fatima Andersen
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Ciudad Amurallada (Walled City) alone was worth the trip, but combined with Plaza Santo Domingo this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Nadia Cohen
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Cartagena is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Ciudad Amurallada (Walled City). The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Liam Johnson
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Our trip to Cartagena was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Las Bóvedas was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Mia Rossi
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Castillo San Felipe de Barajas and City wall sunset walk. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.
